# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';

export const onRequest = ({ request, respond }) => {
  const token = request.cookies.get('auth_token');

  if (!token) {
    return handleUnauthenticated(respond);
  }

  try {
    jwt.verify(token, process.env.SECRET_KEY);
    return handleAuthenticated(respond);
  } catch (error) {
    return handleInvalidToken(respond);
  }
};

const handleUnauthenticated = (respond) => {
  respond({
    status: 401,
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ message: 'Unauthorized' }),
  });
};

const handleAuthenticated = (respond) => {
  // Handle authenticated request logic here...
  respond({
    status: 200,
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ message: 'Authorized' }),
  });
};

const handleInvalidToken = (respond) => {
  respond({
    status: 401,
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ message: 'Invalid Token' }),
  });
};
